The heading of all nodes is `up`.

n2: (c9, c8)

This looks like a specific folding pattern. The string needs to be rotated at a specific node to achieve this shape.
The domain description says: "A rotation (fold) at node n consists of one action rotate and two following passes over to remaining part of string starting at n."
The `rotate` action rotates the heading of node `n` and sets up the first pass cursor `node-first-pass-next`.
The first pass iterates through the connected nodes starting from the neighbor of `n`, updating their headings and clearing their `at` facts.
The second pass iterates through the connected nodes starting from the neighbor of `n`, setting their new `at` facts based on the new headings.
The `rotate` action takes parameters: `(rotate ?n ?r ?fromdir ?todir)`.
The `rotate-first-pass` action takes parameters: `(rotate-first-pass ?nstart ?r ?n1 ?n2 ?n2x ?n2y ?n2dir ?n2setdir)`.
The `rotate-second-pass` action takes parameters: `(rotate-second-pass ?n1 ?n1x ?n1y ?n1dir ?n2 ?n2x ?n2y)`.
